items.title: Google DeepMind's Research Lets an LLM Rewrite Its Own Game Theory Algorithms — And It Outperformed the Experts
items.author: www.marktechpost.com
items.snippet: Google DeepMind has been highly active with new research publications in early 2026. A standout paper from April 2026 introduces AlphaEvolve, an LLM-powered evolutionary coding agent that automates algorithm design for multi-agent reinforcement learning games. It was applied to game-theory frameworks like Counterfactual Regret Minimization (CFR) and Policy Space Response Oracles (PSRO), producing algorithm variants that outperformed hand-designed state-of-the-art baselines — essentially letting

items.title: Google DeepMind on X: Gemma 4 now in Google AI Studio
items.author: x.com
items.snippet: In early April 2026, Google DeepMind's most prominent announcement was the release of Gemma 4, their most capable open model family to date, optimized for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows. Gemma 4 spans from edge-friendly to 31B parameter variants and was released under Apache 2.0, with weights available on HuggingFace, Kaggle, and Ollama. Simultaneously, DeepMind announced native tool-use capabilities enabling autonomous agents to plan, navigate apps, and execute multi-step tasks with u

items.title: GitHub - google-gemini/deprecated-generative-ai-python
items.author: github.com
items.snippet:
The google-gemini GitHub organization (github.com/google-gemini) hosts 40+ repositories and has been actively releasing updates in 2026. The most prominent active project is gemini-cli, an open-source AI agent that brings Gemini directly into the terminal, with daily releases published at UTC 00:00 and stable weekly releases every Tuesday at UTC 20:00. The gemini-cli repository had notable release notes updates in January 2026 covering new features and fixes.

On the SDK side, Google has unified

items.title: Imagen 4.0 API Documentation - Google AI Developers Forum
items.author: discuss.ai.google.dev
items.snippet: Imagen 4 is Google's latest high-performance image generation model available through both the Gemini API and Vertex AI. On the Gemini API side (ai.google.dev), Imagen 4 supports text-to-image generation with five distinct aspect ratios and photography-specific modifiers. It is accessible via the `imagen-4.0-generate-preview-06-06` model ID. On the Vertex AI side, the model is available via the `predict` REST endpoint, supporting parameters such as `sampleCount`, prompt text, and aspect ratio co

items.title: Migrate from Gemini 3 Pro Preview to Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview - Google AI Developers Forum
items.author: discuss.ai.google.dev
items.snippet: The Gemini API has seen significant updates in early 2026. Most notably, Google launched Gemini 3 Pro Preview (gemini-3-pro-preview) as the flagship Gemini 3 series model with state-of-the-art reasoning, multimodal understanding, and agentic/coding capabilities. Alongside it, Computer Use tool support was added to gemini-3-pro-preview and gemini-3-flash-preview. A new gemini-2.5-flash-preview-04-17 was also released, optimized for price-performance with adaptive thinking. Infrastructure improvem

items.title: Full-Stack Vibe Coding: Building Production-Ready Apps with AI Studio, Stitch & Antigravity | Google Cloud - Community
items.author: medium.com
items.snippet: Google AI Studio has launched a full-stack 'vibe coding' experience powered by its Antigravity Agent, enabling developers to build production-ready applications from natural language prompts alone. The platform creates complete full-stack environments by default — a React frontend paired with a Node.js backend — and supports npm packages, secure secrets management, and server-side logic. The Antigravity Agent manages the entire project context across multiple files, propagating changes coherentl

items.title: Google Cloud Next 2025 Wrap Up | Google Cloud Blog
items.author: cloud.google.com
items.snippet: Google Cloud has made several significant AI and Gemini announcements heading into 2026. Gemini 3.1 Pro was released in preview on Vertex AI and Gemini Enterprise, described as a smarter reasoning model building on the Gemini 3 series with improved complex problem-solving capabilities. Alongside this, Google introduced Gemini Enterprise as a dedicated enterprise AI offering, and the Gemini Live API (powered by Gemini 2.5 Flash Native Audio) reached general availability on Vertex AI, enabling mul

items.title: An upgraded dev experience in Google AI Studio - Google Developers Blog
items.author: developers.googleblog.com
items.snippet: Google AI Studio has received significant updates in early 2026, centered around its integrated coding agent called Google Antigravity. The platform now offers a full-stack 'vibe coding' experience that lets developers turn natural language prompts into production-ready applications, including multiplayer apps, database-backed systems, and authenticated web experiences. Firebase integration is proactively offered when the agent detects an app needs persistent storage or login, provisioning Cloud

items.title: Genkit Overview - Open-source AI framework by Google
items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet: Firebase Genkit is an open-source AI development framework by Google that supports JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Python. It provides a unified API for working with multiple AI models (Google Gemini, OpenAI GPT, Anthropic Claude, etc.), built-in developer tools including a local Developer UI for testing and debugging, structured output generation, tool calling, streaming, and multimodal input/output. The Get Started guides walk through project setup, installing packages, configuring API keys, cr

items.title: Get started with Firebase in C++ | Google Codelabs
items.author: codelabs.developers.google.com
items.snippet: Firebase Studio is Google's agentic cloud-based development environment (currently in Preview) for building full-stack AI apps, including games. It integrates Gemini AI for code generation and supports deployment to Firebase Hosting. For game development specifically, Firebase Studio supports 'vibe coding' — building apps through natural language prompts — and there is a dedicated Google Codelab that walks through building a kids' game using Gemini AI, testing it with p5.js, and publishing it to
